Title: Secrets, Rebels, and the Fight to Silence Leek
Summary: Leek Mamer Leek, a community leader from Bor County, is at the center of a national debate regarding his group, the Red Belt, which aims to protect villages and cattle. He claims he was lured to Nairobi and introduced to rebel emissaries, while others accuse a political actor of attempting to use the Red Belt as a private enforcement arm. Observers suggest the state must decide whether to treat Leek as a combatant or a witness.
Keywords: Leek Mamer Leek, Red Belt, Bor County, South Sudan, Rebellion

Title: Holiday Violence Grips Duk County, Fear Surges
Summary: Duk County experienced a surge in violence during the Christmas and New Year period, with three attacks reported in a week, including cattle raids and an ambush that killed one person. The attacks have left residents fearful, and while no group has claimed responsibility, officials suspect youth from the Greater Pibor Administrative Area, highlighting the ongoing fragility of peace efforts in the region.
Keywords: Duk County, cattle raids, violence, South Sudan, Greater Pibor Administrative Area

Title: Border Breakthrough: Yambio Summit’s Bold Plan
Summary: A two-day summit in Yambio addressed simmering border tensions, cattle-related insecurity, and development challenges in Western Equatoria State. Delegates agreed to form a fact-finding committee to investigate land transactions and contested boundaries, aiming to foster unity and economic vitality.
Keywords: Yambio Summit, Border Tensions, Western Equatoria State, Peaceful Coexistence

Title: Gunships Over Jonglei: Civilians Ordered Out
Summary: The South Sudan People’s Defence Forces (SSPDF) have ordered civilians to evacuate areas near SPLA-IO positions in Nyirol, Uror, and Akobo, Jonglei, ahead of imminent military operations. This order follows a recent airstrike in Lankien that killed over twenty-six civilians, raising fears of increased displacement and humanitarian crisis amidst accusations of escalating ethnic conflict.
Keywords: South Sudan, Jonglei, SSPDF, SPLA-IO, civilian evacuation, conflict

Title: Skyfire Over Nyirol: 26 Civilian Lives Lost
Summary: An alleged airstrike in Lankien town, Nyirol County, South Sudan, on December 26, resulted in the deaths of 26 unarmed civilians, including 11 children. Civil society bodies have condemned the attack, attributing it to the South Sudan People’s Defence Forces, and are appealing for emergency humanitarian aid amidst escalating fear and displacement.
Keywords: South Sudan, Jonglei State, airstrike, civilian casualties, humanitarian crisis, Nyirol

Title: Juba Rebel Row: Who Commands South Sudan’s Guns?
Summary: A dispute has erupted within the South Sudan Patriotic Army (SSPA) regarding the legitimacy of forces commanded by agriculture minister Hussein Abdelbagi Akol. Deputy commander Maj. Gen. Deng Mayar Barjok accuses Abdelbagi of presenting civilians as soldiers, while Abdelbagi's wing asserts his authority and political mandate to integrate loyal forces. The disagreement highlights challenges to the 2018 revitalised peace agreement and the planned December 2026 elections.
Keywords: South Sudan, SSPA, Hussein Abdelbagi Akol, Deng Mayar Barjok, peace agreement, elections, military integration

Title: Border Standoff: UPDF Fatal Raid Sparks Crisis
Summary: A South Sudanese military intelligence officer was killed near the Ugandan border, sparking a crisis between South Sudan and Uganda. South Sudan alleges a preplanned incursion by Ugandan forces, while Uganda claims the officer was a thief. The incident has led to diplomatic tensions and calls for renewed border demarcation efforts.
Keywords: border dispute, South Sudan, Uganda, military intelligence, Kajo-Keji

Title: Kon John Akot Elevated: South Sudan Police Shake-Up
Summary: Major Kon John Akot, head of traffic police, has been promoted to Lieutenant General as part of a significant reshuffle within the South Sudan police service. This promotion follows a recent controversy regarding his directives on right-hand-drive vehicles, which were later suspended. Analysts view this as a sign of institutional confidence in his administrative abilities and a potential increase in his authority to influence urban mobility policies.
Keywords: South Sudan, Police, Promotion, Kon John Akot, Traffic

Title: Silent Nights: Western Equatoria’s Safe Christmas
Summary: Western Equatoria experienced a remarkably peaceful Christmas period with no reported crimes, attributed to enhanced cooperation between police, traditional leaders, and youth groups. This success follows years of conflict and displacement in the region, offering cautious optimism for sustainable peace through continued vigilance and community trust.
Keywords: Western Equatoria, South Sudan, Christmas peace, police cooperation, community unity

Title: South Sudan Peace Amendments Spark New Storm
Summary: A proposal to remove Articles 8.2 and 8.3 from South Sudan's peace agreement has sparked debate. Proponents argue it streamlines the path to December elections, while civil society fears it will dismantle enforcement mechanisms and jeopardize fragile reforms. The move raises concerns about accountability, donor confidence, and potential instability ahead of the polls.
Keywords: South Sudan, peace agreement, elections, conflict, governance, reforms

Title: Will Machar’s Release Unlock South Sudan’s Peace?
Summary: The article discusses the uncertain path to peace in South Sudan following a National Breakfast Prayers for Peace and Reconciliation event. Despite calls for inclusive dialogue and the release of First Vice-President Riek Machar, President Salva Kiir offered no specific assurances, leaving many worried about ongoing issues. International partners and activists emphasize the need for practical steps and adherence to the 2018 peace accord to achieve stability and enable democratic elections.
Keywords: South Sudan, Riek Machar, Salva Kiir, peace, reconciliation, elections

Title: Jonglei Alert: SPLM-IO Urges Civilians to Evacuate
Summary: The SPLM-IO has issued a public safety alert in Northern Jonglei, South Sudan, due to renewed clashes with the SSPDF, urging civilians to evacuate or shelter indoors. The group claims to be engaged in "genocide stoppage" and self-defense, appealing for humanitarian assistance and promising access to prisoners of war. These hostilities threaten to worsen displacement and food insecurity in the region.
Keywords: SPLM-IO, SSPDF, Jonglei, South Sudan, conflict, civilian safety, humanitarian crisis
Location: Jonglei; Pieri; Motot; Paluony; Pathai; Waat

Title: Border Oilfield Drama: Heglig Nears a Hot Restart
Summary: South Sudanese engineers have begun returning to the Heglig oilfield, signaling the first step towards restarting production after it briefly fell to Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces. A three-party agreement now sees South Sudan's military safeguarding the installations, with engineers reporting a calm atmosphere despite some RSF presence. The restart is crucial for the economies of both Sudan and South Sudan, with hopes for limited pumping to resume within weeks.
Keywords: Heglig, oilfield, South Sudan, Sudan, Rapid Support Forces, oil production

Title: From Rebellion to Renewal: Mundri East’s Turning Point
Summary: Mundri East, a region in Western Equatoria, is grappling with insecurity caused by unregulated checkpoints, cattle herders, and land disputes, disrupting daily life and eroding trust. Commissioner John Henry Ngalamu advocates for unity and collaboration over rebellion to foster economic revival and social cohesion. Authorities are pledging joint patrols and dialogue, with the community hoping for consistent engagement and tangible improvements.
Keywords: Mundri East, insecurity, land disputes, South Sudan, community conflict

Title: Juba’s Christmas Security Sweep Nets 100 Suspects
Summary: A Christmas Eve security operation in Juba, South Sudan, led to the detention of 100 suspects, including 16 soldiers, for various offenses like disorderly conduct and weapons possession. The sweep involved multiple security forces working to reduce street crime, with authorities assuring transparent judicial processes.
Keywords: Juba, security sweep, Christmas Eve, South Sudan, arrests

Title: Upper Nile Governor’s Christmas Unity Appeal
Summary: Governor Jacob Dollar Ruot of Upper Nile used his Christmas address to advocate for unity, mutual forgiveness, and peaceful coexistence among the diverse communities of Upper Nile. He emphasized that security is crucial for the delivery of essential services and urged politicians to reconcile for the sake of investment and development. The appeal aligns with national stabilization efforts, aiming to restore Malakal's former prosperity and encourage youth to build futures within South Sudan.
Keywords: Upper Nile, South Sudan, peace, unity, governance, development

Title: Christmas Message of Unity Echoes in Juba Parish
Summary: Governor Emmanuel Adil Anthony delivered a Christmas message of unity and peace at St. James Bari Parish in Juba, emphasizing social healing and economic recovery for Central Equatoria. He urged citizens to embrace love, sincerity, and generosity towards vulnerable neighbors as they look towards 2026.
Keywords: Christmas, Juba, unity, peace, Central Equatoria, reconciliation

Title: US Envoy’s Christmas Wish: South Sudan Peace 2026
Summary: US Ambassador Michael J. Adler delivered a Christmas message in Juba, urging South Sudanese leaders and citizens to prioritize peace, dialogue, and reconciliation to make 2026 a landmark year. He emphasized that security is crucial for economic revival and social services, a sentiment echoed by analysts and diplomats. The ambassador highlighted Washington's long-standing support for the South Sudanese people, framing aid and policies around achieving peace.
Keywords: South Sudan, peace, Juba, US Ambassador, 2026, reconciliation

Title: Christmas Clashes In Jonglei Spark Urgent Ceasefire Plea
Summary: Christmas Day in Jonglei, South Sudan, was marred by clashes between the South Sudan People’s Defence Forces, SPLA-IO, and White Army fighters. This violence prompted an urgent plea for a ceasefire from activist Edmund Yakani, who warned of potential escalation during the dry season and its dire impact on civilians and aid efforts amidst shrinking global aid budgets.
Keywords: South Sudan, Jonglei, Ceasefire, Conflict, Christmas

Title: Teen Tragedy Sparks Manhunt in Eastern Equatoria
Summary: A 14-year-old herder, Isidor Arun, was killed by a gunshot while tending cattle in Marguna Payam, Lafon County, Eastern Equatoria. The rifle, carried by 16-year-old Oputo Otong Muna, discharged accidentally. Muna, who was detained, escaped before customary mediation, prompting a manhunt by security forces.
Keywords: Eastern Equatoria, gunshot, herder, manhunt, Lafon County

Title: Sudan Drone Strike Forces UNISFA Exit From Kadugli
Summary: A drone strike in Kadugli on December 13 killed six UN peacekeepers and wounded nine others, leading UNISFA to withdraw all staff and NGO partners from the area. This closure ends nearly thirteen years of support operations and raises concerns about disruptions to cross-border trade and humanitarian aid. UNISFA is continuing its investigation and maintaining smaller sites while awaiting further guidance.
Keywords: Sudan, UNISFA, Kadugli, drone strike, peacekeepers, South Kordofan

Title: Midnight Cattle Raid in Jonglei Leaves 4 Dead
Summary: Four people were killed and 300 cattle stolen in a midnight raid on a herders' camp in Jonglei State, South Sudan. The attack, suspected to be carried out by armed youth from the Greater Pibor Administrative Area, highlights the ongoing issue of cattle raiding and inter-communal violence in the region. Local officials are calling for increased security and early-warning systems.
Keywords: Jonglei State, Cattle Raid, South Sudan, Pibor, Violence

Title: Christmas Appeal: Cardinal Ameyu’s Forgiveness Message
Summary: Cardinal Stephen Ameyu Martin Mulla delivered a powerful Christmas Eve sermon in Juba, urging South Sudanese families to embrace forgiveness and love amidst memories of conflict. His message emphasized reconciliation as a path to lasting peace, resonating with both youth and other religious leaders. The archdiocese plans outreach programs to continue fostering community healing.
Keywords: Cardinal Ameyu, South Sudan, forgiveness, peace, Juba, reconciliation

Title: Christmas Truce? Activist Pressures Kiir to Act
Summary: Civil society leader Edmond Yakani has urged South Sudanese President Salva Kiir to declare a nationwide Christmas ceasefire and release political detainees to foster reconciliation and public confidence. He also cautioned against unilateral amendments to the 2018 peace agreement and the draft constitution, emphasizing the need for consensus among all signatories. Yakani highlighted insecurity, funding issues for electoral bodies, and a restrictive political climate as major impediments to the 2026 elections, advocating for inclusive dialogue and goodwill gestures for stability.
Keywords: South Sudan, ceasefire, political detainees, peace agreement, elections

Title: Bor Clashes: 4 Dead After Firearms Ban Showdown
Summary: Four people were killed in Bor, Jonglei State, after security personnel confronted armed civilians who refused to surrender their weapons. The incident involved a chase and shootout, resulting in the death of one officer and three suspected gunmen. Authorities suspect the civilians were part of the Red Belt vigilante movement and have restored calm to the area while increasing patrols.
Keywords: Bor, Jonglei State, firearms ban, Red Belt, clashes, South Sudan

Title: Seven Hotspots Locked Down for Festive Safety
Summary: Eastern Equatoria State has implemented a pre-holiday security operation in Torit town, deploying mixed forces to seven strategic points to ensure safety during Christmas and New Year. The initiative aims to create a secure environment for celebrations, deterring crime with a unified command and strict discipline among forces. Residents and activists have expressed mixed reactions, with hopes for reduced crime but concerns about detention procedures.
Keywords: Eastern Equatoria State, Torit, security surge, holiday safety, South Sudan

Title: Midnight Blaze in Yambio Leaves Survivor Homeless
Summary: A woman in Yambio, South Sudan, lost her home to an arson attack shortly before midnight on Monday, highlighting a concerning rise in violent crime in the Duduma neighborhood. Police and county officials are implementing increased patrols and joint operations with the army to address the insecurity, while church groups are organizing night vigils. The survivor is receiving temporary shelter as authorities investigate and residents hope for swift arrests to restore peace.
Keywords: Yambio, arson, violent crime, insecurity, Duduma

Title: South Sudan Clergy’s Urgent Christmas Peace Plea
Summary: On Christmas Eve, Archbishop Justin Badi Arama of the South Sudan Council of Churches urged national leaders to prioritize peace and implement the 2018 peace deal, citing widespread suffering and weakened public confidence. He called for the release of political detainees and unconditional dialogue between rival leaders to ensure lasting stability. The Church also requested international assistance for displaced families facing conflict and floods.
Keywords: South Sudan, peace plea, Christmas, Archbishop Justin Badi Arama, political detainees, humanitarian aid

Title: Outcry Over UN Staff Killing in South Sudan
Summary: Bol Rhoch Mayol Koul, a UN language assistant, was killed in South Sudan on December 15, 2025, sparking outrage from the UN and international community. The Transitional Government condemned the killing, pledged swift justice, and announced an arrest, while several embassies issued a joint statement urging accountability and protection for international personnel.
Keywords: South Sudan, UNMISS, Killing, Justice, Humanitarian

Title: Will Honest Talks Save South Sudan’s Fragile Peace?
Summary: South Sudan's 2018 Revitalised Agreement for peace is facing instability due to delayed reforms, contested timelines, and economic hardship. Activist Edmund Yakani emphasizes the need for genuine dialogue within established institutions to address grievances and prevent a return to conflict. He urges regional and international partners to press leaders to uphold their commitments and prioritize compromise for credible elections.
Keywords: South Sudan, peace agreement, conflict, dialogue, security reforms

Title: Bloody Dawn Raid Shakes Warrap Fishing Camp
Summary: Suspected youths from Unity State attacked a fishing camp in Warrap on December 16, killing one and wounding two. The incident highlights ongoing tensions between cattle-keeping communities and has prompted warnings for residents to avoid wetlands. Authorities are also investigating a separate cattle theft incident.
Keywords: Warrap, Unity State, Gogrial East County, fishing camp, cattle looting, inter-communal violence
Location: Warrap; Gogrial East County; Unity State; Malual-Adoor; Wau Teaching Hospital; Mayom; Twic County; Mayen-Amuol; Lakes

Title: UN Staffer Death in Wau: Army Arrests Suspect
Summary: A South Sudanese UN language assistant, Bol Rhoch Mayol Kuol, died in Wau while detained by SSPDF soldiers. The foreign ministry of South Sudan condemned the killing, calling it a breach of national law, and announced the arrest of a suspect. Both national and UN teams are investigating the exact cause of death, with the government reaffirming its commitment to transparency and accountability.
Keywords: Wau, UNMISS, South Sudan, SSPDF, Bol Rhoch Mayol Kuol, investigation

Title: UN Builds $50k Police Academy Transforming Bentiu
Summary: The UN has inaugurated a new $50,000 police academy in Bentiu, South Sudan, aimed at improving the investigative skills and professional conduct of local officers. This initiative, funded by UNMISS, seeks to strengthen the rule of law, protect civilians, and build trust within Unity State through specialized training and community engagement.
Keywords: UNMISS, Bentiu, South Sudan, Police Academy, Security

Title: Grassroots Peace Drive Unveils Home-Grown Fund
Summary: The Civic Engagement and Peacebuilding Initiative Consortium (CEPIC) in Juba, South Sudan, held a forum to discuss peacebuilding achievements and priorities, emphasizing unity, inclusive governance, and home-grown solutions. They launched the Peace Initiative Fund to counter shrinking international grants and ensure continued reconciliation efforts. Despite challenges like budget shortfalls, the consortium aims to expand its reach, especially among youth, to foster lasting peace.
Keywords: peacebuilding, South Sudan, civic engagement, community

Title: Upper Nile Bets on Landmark Peace Gathering
Summary: Upper Nile's Deputy Governor Deng Joh Angok is organizing a landmark peace and reconciliation forum in Malakal to address years of violence and restore stability. The initiative aims to foster collective forgiveness, facilitate inclusive dialogue between community members, and lay the groundwork for economic revival and sustainable resource management, ultimately hoping to unlock the region's full potential.
Keywords: Upper Nile, peace forum, reconciliation, South Sudan, Malakal

Title: Deadly Abyei Strike Forces UN Exit from Kadugli Hub
Summary: The United Nations has withdrawn its main logistics hub from Kadugli after a deadly drone strike killed six Bangladeshi peacekeepers. This attack, attributed to the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese army and the Rapid Support Forces, has raised concerns about humanitarian aid access. UNISFA's core mission of monitoring the disputed border continues, with a potential return to Kadugli if security improves.
Keywords: Abyei, Kadugli, UNISFA, Sudan, South Sudan, drone strike

Title: South Sudan’s New Reformers: How Dr. Chol Deng Thon Abel and Bak Barnaba Chol Are Restoring State Credibility
Summary: South Sudan is undergoing a significant transformation in governance, led by Dr. Chol Deng Thon Abel and Hon. Bak Barnaba Chol. Dr. Chol's diplomatic success in securing the Heglig oilfield and Minister Bak's efforts to restore financial credibility and combat corruption are redefining the country's international image and attracting renewed investor confidence. This new leadership demonstrates a commitment to probity, legitimacy, and competence, signaling a hopeful new chapter for South Sudan.
Keywords: South Sudan, governance reform, oil diplomacy, financial credibility, anti-corruption

Title: Child Soldiers Freed to UN in South Sudan
Summary: Eight child soldiers were freed to the UN Mission in South Sudan by SPLM-IO officials in Lankien. These youths had escaped an SSPDF training site and will receive counselling and medical screening before being reunited with their families. This handover signifies increased cooperation between SPLM-IO and the UN in child protection.
Keywords: child soldiers, South Sudan, UNMISS, SPLM-IO, SSPDF
Location: Lankien; Gelachiel; Nyirol; Bor; Nasir; Ulang

Title: Yambio’s Youth & Women Step Up for Lasting Peace
Summary: A workshop in Yambio, South Sudan, brought together over fifty youth leaders, women representatives, and traditional chiefs for intensive peacebuilding training. Organized by SIHA, the 'From Dialogue to Leadership' project aimed to enhance mediation and advocacy skills, addressing conflict resolution, digital citizenship, and promoting women's voices in decision-making to foster lasting peace in Western Equatoria State.
Keywords: peacebuilding, Yambio, South Sudan, women leadership, conflict resolution, Western Equatoria

Title: South Sudan Police Promise Safe, Hate-Free Christmas
Summary: South Sudan's National Police Service is increasing patrols and implementing security measures across the country to ensure a safe and hate-free Christmas season. Maj. Gen. Monday Anoka emphasized combating hate speech, responsible media reporting, and community involvement to prevent crime and ethnic tensions. The police urge citizens to report suspicious activities and maintain restraint for a peaceful holiday.
Keywords: South Sudan, Christmas, Police, Hate Speech, Security, Juba

Title: Juba Lawmaker’s Killing Rekindles ‘Unknown Gunmen’ Fear
Summary: Luka Mathen Toukpiny Luk, a Council of States member, was shot and killed in his Juba compound, rekindling fears about 'unknown gunmen.' The attack has prompted calls for a transparent security review and swift justice, with authorities reporting arrests and ongoing investigations. The incident highlights deeper issues within South Sudan's security landscape and tests the country's peace roadmap.
Keywords: Juba, Luka Mathen Toukpiny Luk, Unknown Gunmen, South Sudan Security

Title: Spy Gear & Sandalwood: Juba Grounds UN Fleet
Summary: South Sudan has temporarily grounded four planes contracted to the UN Mission in South Sudan due to undisclosed surveillance devices and suspected sandalwood smuggling. An independent committee is investigating the private contractors, not UNMISS itself, to ensure national airspace safety and address environmental violations.
Keywords: South Sudan, UNMISS, surveillance, sandalwood, airspace

Title: Manhunt Looms: Bor County Ultimatum to Red Belt Boss
Summary: Bor County administration has issued an ultimatum to Red Belt commander Leek Mamer, demanding his surrender amidst widespread security sweeps in Jonglei State. County leaders are determined to re-establish government authority and end insecurity, emphasizing that the campaign targets lawbreakers, not communities. Officials are preparing for a manhunt if negotiations for a peaceful surrender fail, while youth leaders urge disarming once effective state protection is guaranteed.
Keywords: Bor County, Red Belt, Leek Mamer, Jonglei State, insecurity, South Sudan

Title: Agenda Dispute Delays South Sudan Peace Talks
Summary: Peace talks in Juba, South Sudan, aimed at discussing a revitalized peace deal, were delayed due to an agenda dispute. Representatives from the SPLM-IG and allied groups walked out, arguing that certain topics were already resolved. Organizers are working to address the concerns to secure broader endorsement for the dialogue.
Keywords: South Sudan, peace talks, Juba, SPLM-IG, agenda dispute

Title: Bank Scandal Suspects Moved for Trial After 2 Years
Summary: Sixteen suspects, detained for nearly two years by National Security, have been transferred to Malakia Police Station to commence standard judicial procedures. They are accused of involvement in illegal transactions that siphoned money from the Central Bank in 2023. This move is seen as vital for transparency and the suspects' rights, allowing for family visits and medical attention, with investigations expected to proceed quickly.
Keywords: Bank Scandal, National Security, Malakia Police Station, Judicial Procedures, Central Bank

Title: Juba’s Stand: UN Bases in Wau and Bentiu Under Fire
Summary: South Sudan's foreign affairs minister, Monday Semaya Kumba, declared the closure of UNMISS hubs in Wau and Bentiu as "non-negotiable," asserting it as a matter of national sovereignty despite ongoing humanitarian concerns. This demand comes as UNMISS undergoes a gradual reduction of peacekeepers due to a system-wide budget crunch, requiring a 15 percent spending cut within six months. The dispute over these strategic bases, critical for humanitarian access, remains unresolved even with a joint technical committee in place.
Keywords: South Sudan, UNMISS, Wau, Bentiu, peacekeeping, sovereignty, humanitarian access

Title: Wau Market Police Patrols Spark Festive Calm
Summary: Extra police patrols in Wau's Jou Market have significantly reduced theft incidents ahead of Christmas, according to local vendors. This increased security has also positively impacted commodity prices and consumer confidence, with officials considering similar deployments elsewhere.
Keywords: Wau, police patrols, theft, market security, South Sudan

Title: Mystery Killing of South Sudan MP Shakes Juba
Summary: Luka Mathen Toukpiny Luk, a South Sudanese MP for Cueibet County, was fatally shot in his Juba compound on Tuesday night after evening prayers. Unidentified assailants breached his gate, and while two suspects are in custody, two accomplices remain at large. The motive for the killing is unknown, raising concerns about the safety of officials in South Sudan.
Keywords: South Sudan, MP, Killing, Juba, Security, Investigation

Title: Lakes State Signs Landmark Cattle Route Peace Pact
Summary: Lakes State recently hosted a two-day forum in Rumbek, bringing together over 200 chiefs, youth leaders, and officials to establish safe dry-season cattle routes. The meeting resulted in a peace pact with clear rules on grazing and water access, aiming to prevent conflict and promote coexistence among communities. This agreement, monitored by youth and traditional chiefs, seeks to end years of cyclical violence by addressing issues like cattle raids and ensuring adherence to a harmonized code.
Keywords: Lakes State, cattle routes, peace pact, Rumbek, conflict resolution, South Sudan

Title: Kenyan Envoy’s Bold Call for South Sudan Peace
Summary: Kenya's ambassador to South Sudan, Jeremy Nyamaso Ndola, called for continued support for the South Sudan peace roadmap during Kenya's 62nd Jamhuri Day reception in Juba. He emphasized that stability is crucial for the country's social and economic future, linking security to inclusive growth, human rights protection, and increased business confidence. Ndola urged regional and international partners to maintain their mediation efforts as the transitional roadmap deadlines approach, expressing optimism that collective backing can help South Sudan achieve peace.
Keywords: South Sudan, Kenya, peace roadmap, diplomacy, Juba

Title: Empty Plane Row: Inside South Sudan’s UN Spat
Summary: A spat between South Sudan and the UN mission (UNMISS) has highlighted mistrust regarding peacekeeper rotations, following a Nepalese rotation flight that returned empty due to a manifest mismatch. While South Sudan's Foreign Minister insists their concerns are not obstructions, the U.S. has warned against interference, threatening aid reductions. This situation has broader implications for regional security.
Keywords: South Sudan, UNMISS, peacekeepers, Juba, United States, aid, security

Title: High-Level Tonj East Tour Renews Warrap Peace Hopes
Summary: A high-level delegation from Warrap State recently visited Tonj East to promote peace, security, and development in the region. The visit aimed to reassure citizens of governmental support, encourage dialogue, and facilitate economic opportunities, building on the legacy of the 1999 Wunlit Peace Conference.
Keywords: Warrap State, Tonj East, Peace, South Sudan, Wunlit Peace Conference

Title: Bor County Ultimatum: Vigilantes Face Army Choice
Summary: In Bor County, the Red Belt vigilantes, who claim to protect villages from cattle raids, have been given an ultimatum by local authorities: join the South Sudan People’s Defence Forces or leave the area. This decision comes after the national army labeled the group a rebel outfit following an incident in September. Officials hope to integrate local defense efforts into state structures, with negotiations and family influence being used to persuade the vigilantes.
Keywords: Bor County, Red Belt vigilantes, South Sudan, SSPDF, cattle raids, negotiation

Title: South Sudan: 21 Women Mediators Drive Peace Talks
Summary: On Human Rights Day, UN Women recognized 21 South Sudanese women mediators for their pivotal role in advancing peace talks and inclusive national dialogue in South Sudan. These mediators received specialized training and successfully facilitated inter-party dialogues, easing tensions and restarting bilateral meetings among signatories to the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement. Their efforts are seen as crucial for building trust and achieving a just and inclusive South Sudan, with potential for their framework to become a model for future reforms.
Keywords: South Sudan, women mediators, peace talks, UN Women, conflict resolution, inclusive dialogue

Title: Is South Sudan Ready to Honor Its Fallen Heroes?
Summary: Twelve years after its independence, South Sudan grapples with persistent violence, displacement, and economic hardship, leading many to feel that the sacrifices of their liberation struggle have been betrayed. The article highlights the need for meaningful remembrance that goes beyond annual ceremonies, advocating for concrete benefits for affected families, inclusive national narratives, and institutional reforms to foster unity and accountability. Ultimately, the country's instability is attributed to unmet obligations and structural weaknesses rather than mystical forces, with a call for South Sudanese leaders to translate agreements into functioning governance.
Keywords: South Sudan, liberation struggle, post-conflict, governance
